analysis_result: ~52.6% (strict) / 56.6% (looser) / PTPRC>0 proxy 52.6% of NF110 post-QC nuclei are immune.
discrepancy_analysis: CONCORDANT within the +/-10% concordance rule. Paper reports 51.2% immune cells in premalignant atypical neurofibroma (G3) samples from snRNA-seq (see main text: 'atypical neurofibromas (G3) (51.2%) had a statistically significant greater representation of immune cell populations'). Model reproduced 52.6% (primary strict estimate, also matching PTPRC>0 proxy) on N=7,499 post-QC NF110 nuclei. Relative difference = |52.6 - 51.2| / 51.2 = 2.73%, well within the +/-10% threshold. The looser estimate of 56.6% is also within ~10.5%, but the primary reported value agrees closely. Minor methodological differences (QC thresholds: model used >=500 genes / <10% mito vs. paper used 1500 / 1.5%) did not materially alter the immune cell proportion estimate.
